title: Spicy Gooey Venezuelan Corn Empanadas
description: Crispy Venezuelan empanadas filled with spicy gooey corn, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This recipe is Jain-friendly and can be made ahead of time for meal prep.

ingredients.name: Spicy corn mix (e.g. frozen, jarred)
ingredients.notes: You can adjust the level of heat according to your preference.

ingredients.name: Active dry yeast
ingredients.notes: You can also use instant yeast.

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine warm water and yeast. Let it sit for 5-7 minutes until frothy.
Add flour, salt, sugar, and 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil to the bowl. Mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1-2 hours until doubled in size.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
Divide the dough into 12 equal pieces and roll each into a ball.
Roll out each ball into a thin circle, about 6 inches in diameter.
Place 1 tablespoon of corn kernels, 1 tablespoon of grated cheese, and 1 tablespoon of spicy corn mix in the center of each circle.
Brush the edges with egg wash and fold the dough in half to form a half-moon shape. Press the edges to seal.
Brush the tops with melted cheese and char-grill until golden brown, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Serve warm with your favorite dipping sauce.
